factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy – Hawkish Surprise
factors.summary: Fed Chair Warsh held rates at 3.50%-3.75% but signaled rate hikes possible this year and removed dovish cut-bias language from statement. Markets repriced to higher terminal rates, pushing DXY to ~100.80 (one-year high per FXStreet). Higher real yields directly compress gold valuations.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: Hawkish Fed and higher nominal yields on no inflation surprise push real yields higher. Lower real yields are the single strongest bullish driver for gold; the inverse is now in play. No explicit TIPS data in news, but yield trajectory is clearly up.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY firmed near 100.80, a one-year high (May 2025), driven by hawkish Fed and higher US rates. Stronger USD is structurally negative for XAUUSD. USD/JPY surged to 161.80 before reversing; broader DXY strength confirmed.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk – US-Iran Peace MoU
factors.summary: US-Iran interim peace deal signed, Strait of Hormuz reopening, Citi forecasts oil $60-65 by Q1 2027. Lower oil/energy prices ease inflation fears, reducing safe-haven demand for gold. Trump claims deal prevents 'global depression,' easing tail-risk premium.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: Oil set for 'deep weekly loss' (Bloomberg) as Hormuz normalizes. Citi commentary emphasizes 're-anchor to weaker fundamentals.' Lower commodity prices reduce inflation expectations, reducing breakeven support for gold. This is deflationary for markets.

factors.name: Japan Inflation & BoJ Signals
factors.summary: Japan May CPI headline 1.5% (expected 1.5%), core 1.4% (expected 1.4%), core-core 1.8% vs expected 1.9%. BoJ Deputy Governor Himino signaled FX moves carry bigger inflation punch; BoJ April minutes showed three members wanted hikes (now validated by 1.00% move). Neutral for gold; BoJ hawkish tilt doesn't offset Fed surprise magnitude.

factors.name: US Equity Rally Post-Deal
factors.summary: Russell 2000 +2.12%, NASDAQ +1.91%, S&P +1.08% as peace deal lifted risk-on sentiment. Risk-on typically means capital flows away from safe-haven gold into equities. Warrant that risk appetite is rising, not falling.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Physical Demand
factors.summary: No flow data in the news window. Seasonality is early-neutral (not peak Indian wedding season yet). No update on CB gold buying (PBoC, RBI, Turkey) in recent 24h.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations & Breakevens
factors.summary: No Michigan survey, 5y5y forward breakevens, or TIPS-implied data in news. Peace deal and oil collapse likely to weigh on 5y5y; early signal but not confirmed.

factors.name: Price Action – Momentum & Phase
factors.summary: Gold down -3.05% in 24h, -0.57% in 4h. This is a Phase 2 move (already underway) post-Fed decision (~36h ago). Move is sustained and directional; late entrants face headwinds into potential pullback/mean reversion.

screening.narrative: Gold is in a structured sell-off following the Fed's hawkish hold and Warsh's removal of dovish language, which repriced US rate expectations higher and pushed DXY to one-year highs (~100.80). Simultaneously, the US-Iran peace MoU eased geopolitical premium and triggered a broad equity rally (Russell 2000 +2.12%, tech +1.9%), shifting capital away from safe havens. Oil collapsed as Hormuz normalizes, reducing inflation expectations and supporting the real-rate headwind. The down move is in Phase 2 (36+ hours post-catalyst) with sustained momentum; a pullback to $4,250–$4,280 should be expected before any fresh short entry. Real yields and DXY direction remain the dominant drivers; until either reverses materially, gold remains under pressure.
catalysts_ahead: US markets closed Friday (Juneteenth holiday) — reduced liquidity/volume
Hong Kong & China markets closed (19 June) — Asia liquidity thin
No major US data releases expected Friday (holiday)
Monday (23 June): US PCE inflation print (core PCE trend critical for real yields)
Fed speakers (Warsh, Waller, Williams) — comms style watch given new regime
60-day Iran MoU negotiating window — any friction in Hormuz would reverse oil rally and support gold
screening.risk_warning: Bias is invalidated if: (1) Real yields reverse sharply lower (e.g., recession panic, soft inflation surprise on Monday), (2) DXY breaks below 100.00 on unexpected dovish Fed pivot or geopolitical escalation, (3) Oil reverses higher (Hormuz disruption, failed Iran deal implementation), or (4) Fed speakers walk back hawkish signals and market reprices to fewer/no hikes in 2026. Thin liquidity Friday (US holiday) could amplify reversals.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended in Phase 2 post-Fed. Wait for a pullback/retest to $4,250–$4,280 resistance before adding shorts; shorts initiated below $4,200 face unfavorable risk/reward. Consider scaling into shorts on any intraday bounce toward $4,230–$4,250, with stops above $4,280.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy
factors.summary: Fed Chair Warsh held rates at 3.5-3.75% and stripped cut-bias language from statement, signaling rate hikes could come this year. Bloomberg and FXStreet both cite 'hawkish Fed stance' as primary driver of gold decline. Removal of dovish guidance is materially negative for zero-yield gold.

factors.name: Real Yields / Nominal Yields
factors.summary: USD at one-year high near 100.80 (highest since May 2025 per FXStreet), implying rising real rate environment. Hawkish Fed repricing lifts real yields, which is the single most negative factor for gold. No specific TIPS yield cited but directionally clear.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength
factors.summary: DXY touched one-year high at 100.80, inversely correlated with gold. EUR/USD pushed to new low at 1.1452, USD/JPY spiked to 161.80 before reversal. Broad dollar strength across pairs compresses gold in dollar terms.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium Fade
factors.summary: US-Iran interim peace deal signed, Strait of Hormuz reopening. Bloomberg: 'Gold Set for Weekly Loss as Hawkish Fed Outweighs Peace Deal.' Safe-haven bid unwinding as war premium evaporates. Citi targets oil at $60-65 by Q1 2027, removing inflation tail risk that supported gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices Collapsing
factors.summary: Bloomberg: 'Oil Set for Deep Weekly Loss as Hormuz Traffic Starts to Inch Up.' Crude at lowest since conflict began. Lower oil reduces inflation expectations, diminishing gold's inflation-hedge appeal and supporting Fed's hawkish stance.

factors.name: Bank of Japan Policy
factors.summary: BoJ hiked to 1.00% this week (retrospectively hawkish per April minutes), but Japan core inflation at 1.4% y/y (in-line, May data) shows modest pressure. BoJ Deputy Governor warned yen moves carry bigger inflation punch. USD/JPY volatility (spike to 161.80, sharp reversal) creates cross-current but doesn't materially offset USD strength globally.

factors.name: Fed Communication Regime Shift
factors.summary: Warsh skipped dot plot, announced task forces to overhaul Fed operations including communications. InvestingLive: 'After 15 years of word-watching, markets may benefit from a Fed that talks less' but also notes 'markets are only beginning to price the difference.' Increased policy uncertainty leans hawkish as markets price worst-case.

factors.name: Gold Technical / Momentum
factors.summary: Gold down -3.05% in 24h, Bloomberg notes 'third weekly loss' for gold. FXStreet: 'Gold declines to near $4,200 as hawkish Fed stance offsets support from US-Iran peace deal.' Move already underway but not yet extended to multi-year lows—still in Phase 2.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: US equities rallied Thursday (Russell 2000 +2.12%, Nasdaq +1.91%) on Iran peace deal relief, reducing safe-haven demand for gold. However, equity strength driven by geopolitical relief, not growth optimism, so not durably negative for gold. Mixed signal.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No fresh news on PBoC, RBI, or other central bank purchases in the 24h window. Structural bid remains but not a near-term catalyst.

factors.name: Inflation Data
factors.summary: Japan May CPI in-line (headline 1.5% vs 1.5% exp, core 1.4% vs 1.4% exp). No major US inflation prints in window. Absence of hot US inflation data removes bullish catalyst but doesn't add bearish pressure. Next catalyst needed.

confirmation.narrative: Gold is under pressure from a confluence of hawkish Fed repricing (Warsh removed cut bias, signaled potential hikes), rising real yields, and a US dollar rally to one-year highs. Simultaneously, the US-Iran peace deal is unwinding the geopolitical risk premium that supported gold during the Hormuz crisis, with oil collapsing and safe-haven flows reversing. The -3.05% 24h decline reflects Phase 2 of this repricing—move is underway but not yet exhausted. Near-term catalysts are sparse (US markets closed Friday for Juneteenth), allowing the hawkish Fed narrative to dominate.
catalysts_ahead: US markets closed Friday June 19 (Juneteenth)—thin liquidity may exaggerate moves
Next major catalyst: US CPI/PCE data (dates not specified in news but critical for Fed path)
60-day US-Iran negotiation window—any breakdown would reverse geopolitical fade
Fed speaker calendar (Warsh communication style still being decoded by markets)
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Iran deal collapses and Hormuz disruptions resume, spiking oil and safe-haven demand; (2) Fed speakers walk back hawkish interpretation of Wednesday's statement; (3) US inflation data surprises hot, forcing Fed to delay hikes and weakening dollar; (4) USD/JPY intervention or coordinated G7 FX action weakens dollar broadly. Also, gold has already moved -3% in 24h—chasing here is late; better entry on bounce to $4,250-4,280 resistance zone.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move is Phase 2 (underway, -3.05% in 24h). Avoid chasing current level; wait for bounce to $4,250-4,280 resistance to enter shorts, or for break below $4,180 support with confirmation to add to bearish positioning.

reason: DISAGREEMENT: Haiku=SELL vs Sonnet=BEARISH — silent log
